title: China Shifts to South American Soy, U.S. Corn Rises to Japan
section_path: China Shifts to South American Soy, U.S. Corn Rises to Japan
section_path_text: China Shifts to South American Soy, U.S. Corn Rises to Japan
summary:
Main
WEEKLY DRY MARKET MONITOR Spot Rates | Supply-Demand Trends | Port Congestion Overview
Week 36, 2025 | Date: Sept 03, 2025
Image reference: Στιγμιότυπο οθόνης 2025-09-04 125729.png | assets/2025-09-04_china-shifts-to-south-american-soy-us-corn-rises-to-japan_img_cea3cf84ceb9ceb3cebcceb9cf8ccf84cf85_7c97e35b1aab.png
This week we take a closer look at the shifting grain flows of 2025, as U.S.-China trade tensions continue to dominate global perspectives. Agriculture remains at the core of these frictions, with China's move away from U.S. soybeans becoming increasingly evident and Brazil solidifying its role as the leading supplier.
